title: torilis arvensis var. heterophylla
intro: Torilis arvensis var. heterophylla, also known as wild chervil or lesser burnet, is a biennial or perennial plant of the carrot family (Apiaceae) with fern-like foliage and a tall stem bearing small white flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of wild chervil include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe the nervous system, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Wild chervil can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used in cooking or as a natural remedy for various ailments.
